frozen_components: language model agents used for CoT generation
architecture.summary: A knowledge editing system that trains LLMs to reason over edited knowledge using chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. The pipeline: (1) language model agents generate CoTs for both structured (fact triples) and unstructured (news, articles) edited data to create instruction data; (2) the target model is trained via supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to reason over edited knowledge; (3) at inference, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) dynamically retrieves relevant edited facts and provides them as context for the model to perform real-time knowledge editing.
trained_components: LLM fine-tuned via SFT and GRPO on CoT reasoning tasks over edited knowledge

biblio.title: Learning to Edit Knowledge via Instruction-based Chain-of-Thought Prompting

claims.statement: Our method achieves strong generalization across six diverse knowledge editing scenarios with just a single round of training on three open-source language models
Current knowledge editing approaches face poor generalization: most approaches rigidly inject new knowledge without ensuring that the model can use it effectively to solve practical problems
Current knowledge editing methods focus primarily on structured fact triples, overlooking the diverse unstructured forms of factual information (e.g., news, articles) prevalent in real-world contexts
CoT2Edit leverages language model agents for both structured and unstructured edited data to generate CoTs, building high-quality instruction data
